quizzes.title: Python Fundamentals Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of Python basics, environment setup, and first programs.

questions.question: What is the correct file extension for Python scripts?
questions.explanation: Python scripts use the .py file extension.

questions.question: Which command creates a virtual environment in Python?
questions.explanation: The standard way is 'python -m venv venv' (or 'python3 -m venv venv').

questions.question: What is the output of: print('Hello' + 'World')?
questions.explanation: String concatenation with + joins without spaces.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid variable name in Python?
questions.explanation: Underscore-starting names are valid; others have syntax errors.

questions.question: What does the 'type()' function do?
questions.explanation: type() returns the data type of an object.

quizzes.title: Control Flow Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of conditionals, loops, and logical operators.

questions.question: What does 'elif' stand for?
questions.explanation: 'elif' is short for 'else if'.

questions.question: Which loop executes at least once?
questions.explanation: Python has no built-in do-while; while/for may execute zero times.

questions.question: What does 'break' do?
questions.explanation: 'break' immediately exits the nearest enclosing loop.

questions.question: What is the result of: True and False?
questions.explanation: Logical AND returns False if any operand is False.

questions.question: Which operator is used for identity comparison?
questions.explanation: 'is' checks if two references point to the same object.

quizzes.title: Data Structures Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of lists, tuples, dictionaries, and sets.

questions.question: Which data structure is mutable?
questions.explanation: Lists are mutable; tuples, strings, and frozensets are immutable.

questions.question: How do you access the last element of a list 'arr'?
questions.explanation: Negative indices count from the end: -1 is last element.

questions.question: What does a set do?
questions.explanation: Sets store unique, unordered elements.

questions.question: Which method removes and returns the last element of a list?
questions.explanation: pop() removes and returns the last (or specified) element.

questions.question: What is the output of: dict([('a',1),('b',2)])?
questions.explanation: dict() constructor creates a dictionary from an iterable of key-value pairs.

quizzes.title: Functions & Modular Programming Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of functions, arguments, modules, and packages.

questions.question: What keyword defines a function?
questions.explanation: Python uses 'def' to define functions.

questions.question: What does *args allow?
questions.explanation: *args collects extra positional arguments as a tuple.

questions.question: What is the purpose of __name__ == '__main__'?
options: Check if script is run directly
questions.explanation: It allows code to run only when the file is executed as a script, not when imported.

questions.question: Which statement imports a specific function from a module?
questions.explanation: 'from module import function' imports just that function.

questions.question: What is a docstring?
options: String literal as first statement in function/class/module
questions.explanation: A docstring is a string literal that appears as the first statement in a function, class, or module.

quizzes.title: Object-Oriented Programming Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your understanding of classes, objects, inheritance, and OOP concepts.

questions.question: What is the first parameter of instance methods by convention?
questions.explanation: Python uses 'self' as the first parameter of instance methods.

questions.question: What does super() do?
questions.explanation: super() returns a temporary object of the parent class to allow method calls.

questions.question: Which method is called when an object is converted to string with str()?
questions.explanation: __str__ is called by str() and print() for user-friendly string representation.

questions.question: What does a property decorator do?
questions.explanation: @property turns a method into a read-only attribute; can pair with setter.

questions.question: What is duck typing?
questions.explanation: Duck typing means object suitability is determined by presence of methods/attributes, not explicit type.
